snippets.title: k-fold cross-validation with scikit-learn
snippets.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score, KFold
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.datasets import load_diabetes
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X, y = load_diabetes(return_X_y=True)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("scale", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", Ridge(alpha=1.0)),
])

cv = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
scores = cross_val_score(pipe, X, y, cv=cv, scoring="neg_root_mean_squared_error")
print("RMSE folds:", (-scores).round(3))
print("mean:", (-scores).mean().round(3), "std:", (-scores).std().round(3))
snippets.explanation: Pipeline ensures StandardScaler fits on each training fold only. `cross_val_score` returns one score per fold; negate `neg_*` metrics for positive RMSE.

snippets.title: Stratified k-fold for classification
snippets.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, cross_validate
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer

X, y = load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)

clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=0)
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0)

out = cross_validate(
    clf, X, y, cv=cv,
    scoring=["accuracy", "roc_auc"],
    return_train_score=True,
)
print("val accuracy:", out["test_accuracy"].mean().round(3))
print("val roc_auc:", out["test_roc_auc"].mean().round(3))
snippets.explanation: StratifiedKFold preserves class ratios per fold. `return_train_score=True` helps spot overfitting when train ≫ val.

snippets.title: Manual fold loop with preprocessing discipline
snippets.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score
import numpy as np

X = np.random.randn(200, 5)
y = (X[:, 0] + X[:, 1] > 0).astype(int)

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=0)
f1s = []

for train_idx, val_idx in kf.split(X):
    scaler = StandardScaler().fit(X[train_idx])
    X_tr = scaler.transform(X[train_idx])
    X_va = scaler.transform(X[val_idx])
    model = LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000).fit(X_tr, y[train_idx])
    pred = model.predict(X_va)
    f1s.append(f1_score(y[val_idx], pred))

print("F1 per fold:", np.round(f1s, 3))
print("mean:", np.mean(f1s).round(3))
snippets.explanation: Explicit loops make leakage visible: scaler and model must be refit inside each iteration on train_idx only.
